---
title: "sampleScript"
author: "Krista Longnecker"
date: "2026-06-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r startClean, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Clean up the environment
rm(list = ls())

# Set options to prevent automatic conversion of strings to factors
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

```


```{r load libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(data.table) # you need to install this package from CRAN
library(dplyr)
library(readr) #this is part of the tidyverse package
library(here) #install this (once, install.packages("here))
library(lubridate) #great for handling date data
library(zoo) #for interpolations
library(oce) #for integrations (package that will do oceanographic calculations)
library(ggplot2)

library(reshape2)
library(MBA)
library(mgcv)
library(marmap)
library(FNN)

```


```{r readInData, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Load in the sample data file
file.path <- 'sampleData.csv'
data <- read.csv(file.path, header = TRUE)

```

``` {r tidyData}

#Need Id : cruise / cast / niskin that get parsed out here and used later
i <- which(colnames(data)=='BATS_id')
colnames(data)[i] <- 'Id'

# Replace -999 values with NA and extract month and year
data[data == -999] <- NA
data$month <- month(ymd(data$yyyymmdd))
data$year <- year(ymd(data$yyyymmdd))

# Extract cruise, cast, and bottle IDs: need this to match the MATLAB 
# derived variables (mixed layer depths and seasons)
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    cruise = substr(Id, 1, 5),
    cast = as.integer(substr(Id, 6, 8)),
    bottle = as.integer(substr(Id, 9, 10)))


```


``` {r sourceCode}

source("makeSynoptic_script_v1.R")

```


```{r useFunction, warning=FALSE}
# Split data into separate cruises and create synoptic profiles
useDepthLine <- 250   #what depth (m) do you want to use to split shallow versus deep casts?
oneVariable <- 'TOC'  #what variable do you want to get additional information about?

# Divide up the list and get the average values for each cruise
result.list <- lapply(split(data, data$cruise), synoptic.func, useDepthLine, oneVariable)

# Combine the results into one data.table
result <- rbindlist(result.list, fill = TRUE)

# Correct the month column to ensure whole numbers
result$month <- as.numeric(substr(result$yyyymmdd, 5, 6))

# Clean up unnecessary objects
rm(result.list, data, synoptic.func)

```


```{r exportResult}

#make the columns into characters - the cast column is a list, which makes exporting a bit messy
write.table(apply(result,2,as.character),file = "exportResult.csv", sep = ",", row.names = FALSE)


```


# ``` {r makeTest, eval = FALSE}
# 
# fName <- "BATS_CTDdata_2db.2025.04.11.csv"
# df <- read.csv(fName, header = TRUE)
# raw <- df[1:8855,]
# write.csv(raw,"sampleData.csv",row.names = FALSE)
# 
# ```
